title: Azure Logic Apps - Trigger Types With Exact JSON From Real Sentinel Playbooks
description: All trigger types found in real-world Sentinel playbook examples: incident trigger, alert trigger, recurrence, HTTP request, and manual.

sentinel_incident_trigger.description: The primary trigger for Microsoft Sentinel playbooks. Fires when a Sentinel incident is created. Type is ApiConnectionWebhook. The trigger name in the designer is 'Microsoft_Sentinel_incident'.

key_fields.type: ApiConnectionWebhook — this is a webhook-style trigger that registers a callback URL with Sentinel
key_fields.callback_url: @{listCallbackUrl()} — Logic Apps built-in function that returns the webhook callback URL for this run
key_fields.path: /incident-creation — the Sentinel connector endpoint that registers the webhook
key_fields.connection_key: azuresentinel — the key used in $connections to reference the Sentinel connection

connection_key_variants: azuresentinel — most common, used for service-account authenticated connections
azuresentinel_1 — suffix variant when multiple Sentinel connections exist
azuresentinel-1 — hyphen variant seen in some exports
microsoftsentinel — newer connector name, used in updated playbooks
azuresentinel_1 — when trigger uses MSI connection distinct from action connections

what_is_available_downstream.triggerBody(): The full incident payload. All incident data is accessed via @triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?[...]

sentinel_alert_trigger.description: Alert-level trigger (not incident-level). Fires for each individual Sentinel alert. Found in Suppress_Tenable_SAMR_alerts.txt. Path is '/subscribe' not '/incident-creation'.

what_is_available_downstream.description: Alert-level trigger body is different from incident-level. Fields are at top level of triggerBody().

recurrence_trigger.description: Schedule-based trigger. Fires on a defined interval. No connector needed.
recurrence_trigger.what_is_available_downstream: No trigger body. Use @utcNow() to get current time.

http_request_trigger.description: HTTP/request trigger. Exposes an HTTPS endpoint. Fires when a POST is received. Generates a URL after first save.
notes: The 'schema' property is optional but provides IntelliSense in downstream expression picker
Without a schema, use @triggerBody()?['fieldName'] with null-safe ?[''] navigation
The generated URL includes a SAS token — treat it as a secret

trigger_naming_convention.description: The trigger key name in the 'triggers' object becomes the trigger's identity. In downstream expressions, the trigger is always referenced as triggerBody() or triggerOutputs() — never by name.
